# NI DOCUMENT BUNDLE: ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref

<!--NI_BUNDLE_CHUNK bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref start=1 end=250 -->
<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-device-name.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00001: DAQmx Device Name

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-device-name.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-device-name.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all devices installed in the system. Right-click the constant, and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the devices that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is

### DAQmx Device Name

Lists all devices installed in the system. Right-click the constant, and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the devices](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxdevnamefilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and is not a DAQmx Device Property Node.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-device-name.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx System Setup

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-global-channel.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00002: DAQmx Global Channel

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-global-channel.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-global-channel.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all virtual channels you create and save using the DAQ Assistant. Select Browse to select multiple channels. Right-click the constant and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the channels that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you ri

### DAQmx Global Channel

Lists all [virtual channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) you create and save using the [DAQ Assistant](/csh?context=nidaqmx_expdaqmx_daqassistant). Select **Browse** to select multiple channels. Right-click the constant and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxchanfilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant.

If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and is not a DAQmx Channel Property Node.

You can launch the DAQ Assistant from the DAQmx global channel constant to [create a new virtual channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_creatingvirtualchannels) or to [edit a saved virtual channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_editingvirtualchannels).

You can use the DAQmx global channel constant to [generate code for global channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_codegen).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-global-channel.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-physical-channel.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00003: DAQmx Physical Channel

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-physical-channel.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-physical-channel.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all physical channels on devices installed in the system. Select Browse to select multiple physical channels. Right-click the constant, and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the physical channels that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant

### DAQmx Physical Channel

Lists all [physical channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) on devices installed in the system. Select **Browse** to select multiple physical channels. Right-click the constant, and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the physical channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxphyschanfilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant.

If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and does not provide any configuration options for physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-physical-channel.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-scale-name.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00004: DAQmx Scale Name

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-scale-name.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-scale-name.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all custom scales you create and save by using the DAQ Assistant. Right-click the constant, and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the scales that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you right-click this constant and create a Propert

### DAQmx Scale Name

Lists all [custom scales](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales) you create and save by using the [DAQ Assistant](/csh?context=nidaqmx_expdaqmx_daqassistant). Right-click the constant, and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the scales](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxscalefilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant.

If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and is not a DAQmx Scale Property Node.

You can launch the DAQ Assistant from the DAQmx scale name constant to [create a new custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_creatingscales) or to [edit an existing custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_editingscales).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-scale-name.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-task-name.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00005: DAQmx Task Name

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-task-name.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-task-name.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all tasks you create and save by using the DAQ Assistant. You cannot use this constant to select multiple tasks. Right-click the constant, and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the tasks that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you

### DAQmx Task Name

Lists all [tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) you create and save by using the [DAQ Assistant](/csh?context=nidaqmx_expdaqmx_daqassistant). You cannot use this constant to select multiple tasks. Right-click the constant, and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxtaskfilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant.

If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and is not a DAQmx Task Property Node.

You can launch the DAQ Assistant from a DAQmx task name constant to [create a new task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_creatingtasks) or to [edit a saved task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_editingtasks).

You can use a DAQmx task name constant to [generate code](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_codegen) for tasks.

You can [convert](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_convertingtoexpressvi) a DAQmx task name constant to a [DAQ Assistant Express VI](/csh?context=nidaqmx_expdaqmx_daqassistant).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-task-name.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=constants/daqmx-terminal.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00006: DAQmx Terminal

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `constants/daqmx-terminal.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/constants/daqmx-terminal.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. Select Browse to select multiple terminals. Right-click the constant and select I/O Name Filtering from the shortcut menu to limit the terminals that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant. If you right-

### DAQmx Terminal

Lists all [terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_terminal) available on devices installed in the system. Select **Browse** to select multiple terminals. Right-click the constant and select **I/O Name Filtering** from the shortcut menu to [limit the terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_mxtermfilter) that the constant displays and to limit what you can enter in the constant.

If you right-click this constant and create a Property Node, the Property Node is for the constant itself and does not provide any configuration options for terminals.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-terminal.png']

Parent topic:

DAQmx Export Signal

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=lvdaqmxsupp/academic_others.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00007: Academic Devices and Others

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `lvdaqmxsupp/academic_others.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/lvdaqmxsupp/academic_others.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: This section contains lists of the properties you can use with academic devices and other devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

### Academic Devices and Others

This section contains lists of the properties you can use with academic devices and other devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

Parent topic:

Supported Properties by Device

## TOPIC 00036: CompactDAQ Devices

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `lvdaqmxsupp/cdaq.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/lvdaqmxsupp/cdaq.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: This section contains lists of the properties you can use with CompactDAQ devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

### CompactDAQ Devices

This section contains lists of the properties you can use with CompactDAQ devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

Supported Properties by Device

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: This section contains lists of the properties you can use with CompactRIO devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

### CompactRIO Devices

This section contains lists of the properties you can use with CompactRIO devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

## TOPIC 00061: Digital I/O Devices

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `lvdaqmxsupp/dio.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/lvdaqmxsupp/dio.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: This section contains lists of the properties you can use with Digital I/O devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

### Digital I/O Devices

This section contains lists of the properties you can use with Digital I/O devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

Parent topic:

Supported Properties by Device

## TOPIC 00069: FieldDAQ Devices

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `lvdaqmxsupp/fd.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/lvdaqmxsupp/fd.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: This section contains lists of the properties you can use with FieldDAQ devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.

### FieldDAQ Devices

This section contains lists of the properties you can use with FieldDAQ devices NI-DAQmx supports. For some devices, you can use the properties that device supports and the properties supported by another device to which you connect the first device.
